Add usage examples to each CLI command's help output â€” e.g., session shows 'generalstaff session --budget=480  # overnight 8-hour run', cycle shows 'generalstaff cycle --project=myapp --dry-run'. Update the main --help too.
In dispatcher.ts, add a tiebreaker to scoreProjects: when two projects have the same score, prefer the one with fewer total_cycles (spread work more evenly). Add a test for this behavior.
In session.ts, if a cycle returns verification_failed, log a one-line summary to stderr with the reason so the user sees it even when output is scrolling. Add test.

Add a test that validates the full CLI help output includes all registered commands: session, cycle, status, stop, start, log, projects, init, history, doctor, clean. Ensures new commands don't get forgotten in help text.
Add a session.test.ts with tests for the writeDigest function: verify it creates the digest file, includes all cycle results, formats SHA ranges, and handles empty results array

In dispatcher.ts, add a function estimateSessionPlan(projects, fleet, budgetMinutes) that returns a preview of which projects would be picked and how many cycles each would get, without executing anything. Add test.
Add a formatDuration helper function to a new src/format.ts module that converts seconds to human-readable strings (45s, 2m30s, 1h15m). Use it in history and digest output. Add tests.
In work_detection.ts, add a function countRemainingWork(project) that returns the number of pending tasks (not just boolean hasMoreWork). Use it in the session summary output. Add test.
Add a projects.test.ts test that validates warnProjectPaths with a real git repo (use the GeneralStaff repo itself as the valid path) and a non-git directory

Wire estimateSessionPlan into the session command â€” before running cycles, print a preview table showing which projects will be picked and how many cycles each gets. Only show when not --dry-run. Add to session output.
Add a generalstaff task command to cli.ts with subcommands: 'task list --project=<id>' shows pending tasks, 'task add --project=<id> <title>' appends a new task to tasks.json. Include tests.
In the history command output, add ANSI color codes: green for verified, red for verification_failed, yellow for verified_weak, gray for cycle_skipped. Only colorize when stdout is a TTY.
Add a session.test.ts test that validates the empty-cycle guard exits after 3 consecutive empty diffs, and that capped projects get added to the skip set

In audit.ts, add a function summarizeCosts that counts reviewer_invoked events from PROGRESS.jsonl and estimates total token usage based on prompt_length. Wire into the history --format=compact output as an optional column.

Add a generalstaff summary command to cli.ts that prints a one-screen overview: total cycles across all projects, verified/failed/skipped counts, total duration, test count (run bun test --reporter=json to get count), and tasks remaining. Like a dashboard snapshot.

In projects.ts, add a validateHandsOff function that checks each hands_off pattern against the project's actual file tree (git ls-files) and warns about patterns that don't match anything â€” helps users catch typos in their hands-off config. Add test.
Add a test for extractChangedFiles in cycle.ts â€” verify it handles renames (diff --git a/old b/new), binary files, and files with spaces in their names
In state.ts, add a getProjectSummary function that aggregates fleet state + project state + remaining tasks into a single object. Use it in the status --json output for richer data. Add test.
Add a generalstaff version command (separate from --version flag) that shows version, bun version, platform, and projects.yaml location. Useful for bug reports.

In cycle.ts, add a diffSummaryStats function that parses the diff to count files changed, insertions, and deletions. Include these stats in the cycle_end PROGRESS entry and the digest output. Add test.

Add a generalstaff config command to cli.ts that pretty-prints the current projects.yaml config (parsed and validated) â€” shows each project with all resolved defaults. Useful for debugging config issues. Add test.

In work_detection.ts, add support for a third work_detection mode 'git_issues' that counts open issues on the project's git remote via 'git log --oneline origin/master..HEAD' as a proxy for pending work. Add test with mock.
Add a test for the session plan preview (estimateSessionPlan) with multiple projects at different priorities â€” verify the picker alternates correctly and respects the per-project cap
In format.ts, add a formatBytes helper (1024 -> 1.0 KB, 1048576 -> 1.0 MB) for displaying diff sizes and log file sizes in the history and summary commands. Add tests.

In src/clean.ts, add a log-rotation step: delete files in logs/ older than N days (N configurable via --log-days=<n> flag, default 30). Surface count of deleted files in the clean output. Add tests covering files on both sides of the threshold and a custom --log-days override.

Add a 'generalstaff digest' command to cli.ts that cats a digest file to stdout. Support --latest (most recent file in digests/) and --date=YYYYMMDD (first digest from that day). If no digests exist, print a clear message. Add tests.

In src/session.ts, improve live console output between cycles: after each cycle, print 'Cycle N completed: <project> â€” <outcome>' and the remaining budget in minutes (use formatDuration). Makes interactive observation of long sessions readable. Add a test that captures stderr/stdout and asserts the format.
In src/summary.ts, add a Disk Usage section to the fleet summary dashboard: total bytes in logs/, digests/, and state/. Use formatBytes for display. Handle missing directories (0 B). Add test.

Add a test pinning formatBytes behavior for fractional sub-KB inputs (e.g. 1023.5 bytes). Either keep current behavior (returns '1023.5 B') and add an explicit test documenting it, or change the implementation to floor sub-KB values and update existing tests accordingly. Include a comment in format.ts explaining the chosen convention.

Add a 'generalstaff digest --list' subcommand that enumerates all digest files in digests/ with their date, total cycles, and verified/failed/skipped breakdown (parsed from the digest markdown). If no digests exist, print a clear message. Sort newest first. Add tests covering empty dir, one digest, and multiple digests.

In the 'history' command, add --since=YYYYMMDD and --until=YYYYMMDD filter flags that filter cycles by started_at. Reject malformed date input with a clear error (not a silent empty result). Add tests covering valid ranges, out-of-range filters, and malformed input.

Add --format=json to 'generalstaff summary' that emits the FleetSummary object as JSON (same data as the text dashboard, machine-readable). Include tests verifying JSON parses and matches the text output's counts.

Add CLI subcommand 'generalstaff task done --project=<id> --task=<task-id>' to mark a single task as done without hand-editing tasks.json. Error if project or task not found. Print a notice (don't error) if already done. Add tests for success, not-found, already-done.

In tasks.ts loadTasks, surface JSON parse errors instead of silently returning an empty list (current behavior misleads users into thinking there's 'no work' when really their tasks.json is malformed). On parse failure, log a clear error with the file path and rethrow or return a typed failure. Update callers to handle the new failure mode gracefully. Add tests covering malformed JSON, empty file, and valid-but-not-array structure.

Add --max-cycles=N flag to 'generalstaff session' as an alternative stopping condition alongside --budget. If both specified, stop at whichever hits first. Log which limit triggered ('max-cycles' vs 'budget'). Add tests covering max-cycles-hits-first, budget-hits-first, and default (only --budget).

In audit.ts summarizeCosts, add per-project breakdown of reviewer_invoked events (currently aggregated across fleet). Return { byProject: Record<string, {invocations, estTokens}>, total: {...} }. Wire into 'history --costs' output as an extra column when --project is not specified. Add tests.

Add --json flag to 'generalstaff digest' (pair with --list/--latest/--date from gs-057). Emits the same data as the markdown digest but as a structured JSON object (duration, cycle count, per-cycle results array with outcome/sha/diff_stats). Add tests.

Add test coverage for countCommitsAhead in cycle.ts. Use a tmp git repo fixture: init repo, make a commit on master, create a branch, verify count=0. Add a commit on the branch, verify count=1. Merge branch into master, verify count=0. Also verify returns 0 when branch doesn't exist.

Add 'generalstaff bot-status [--project=<id>]' CLI command that prints the bot branch's commit count ahead of HEAD for each project (using countCommitsAhead). Indicates pending unmerged work. Format: 'myproject: 2 commit(s) on bot/work not yet on master'. Add tests.

In work_detection.ts, add a 'git_unmerged' mode that counts commits on bot/work ahead of master as a proxy for pending work (alternative to tasks_json for projects without an explicit task file). Add VALID_WORK_DETECTION entry, cycle.ts dispatch, and tests.
Add a formatPercent helper to src/format.ts (0.42 -> '42%', 1.0 -> '100%', 0 -> '0%', handles NaN/Infinity/negatives by returning '?'). Include tests covering boundaries and invalid inputs.

In src/summary.ts, add a per-project success-rate line to the Cycles section of the fleet summary: 'Success rate: NN% (X/Y verified)' using formatPercent. Only shown when a project has at least one cycle. Add test.

Add a 'generalstaff log --level=error' filter that shows only cycle_skipped, verification_failed, and entries with event names ending in _error from PROGRESS.jsonl. Pair with existing --lines/--project flags. Add tests.

Add test coverage for the OpenRouter reviewer code path in reviewer.ts. Mock the global fetch to return a well-formed chat-completion response; verify invokeOpenRouterReviewer builds the correct request body (model, messages, temperature), parses the response, and fail-safes correctly on HTTP errors and malformed JSON.

In state.ts, add a getRecentCycles(projectId, n) helper that returns the last N cycle results by reading PROGRESS.jsonl (cycle_end events only, newest first). Wire into the status --json output so the JSON includes a 'recent_cycles' array. Add tests.
Add a 'generalstaff history --verified-only' flag that filters out cycle_skipped and verification_failed rows from the history output. Works in both default and --format=compact modes. Add tests.

Improve session console output to include an ETA: after each cycle completes, print 'Cycle N completed ... (projected end: HH:MM)'. Projected end = now + (remaining cycles estimate * avg cycle duration). Skip ETA if fewer than 2 cycles have completed (need a sample). Add test.

Add a test for extractChangedFiles in cycle.ts that covers the --diff-filter=A added-only case: given a diff with only new files and no modifications, verify the function returns the added file paths correctly.

In src/audit.ts, add a function loadProgressEvents(projectId, filterFn) that returns a filtered array of parsed ProgressEntry objects from PROGRESS.jsonl. Use it to refactor existing readCycleEnds/summarizeCosts for consistency. Add tests covering filter matches, empty file, malformed lines.

Add a docs section to CLAUDE.md (Session context) documenting the OpenRouter reviewer opt-in (env vars GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER and OPENROUTER_API_KEY, where the key is sourced from on Ray's machines, how the .bat wrapper sets them). Update the memory-discipline note's cross-references.

In src/projects.ts, add a getProject(id) lookup helper with a typed 'not found' error that includes the list of available project ids. Refactor existing string-match fallbacks (like in cli.ts task list) to use it. Add tests.

Add a 'generalstaff digest --latest' flag that prints the absolute path of the most recently modified digest file in digests/, or a clear message if no digest exists. Add tests covering the happy path, empty directory, and filter compatibility with --json/--list.

Add a 'generalstaff summary --project=<id>' filter flag that restricts the fleet summary dashboard output to a single project. When unknown project id is passed, print a clear error listing valid ids (use getProject helper). Add tests.

Add a formatRelativeTime(iso: string, now?: Date) helper to src/format.ts that converts an ISO timestamp to a human relative string: 'just now' (<60s), '3 min ago', '2h ago', 'yesterday at HH:MM', 'N days ago'. Handles future dates gracefully ('in N min'). Include tests covering each bucket and invalid input.

Wire formatRelativeTime (from gs-085) into the 'generalstaff status' command output: replace the raw ISO timestamp on the 'Last cycle: ...' line with a relative version, but keep the ISO in parentheses for precision. Add tests. Only proceed after gs-085 lands.

Add a test for parseDigest in src/session.ts that verifies compatibility with the new multi-section digest format (## What got done / ## Issues / ## Details headers before the per-cycle sections). The existing cycle-section regex expects '## <project> â€” <cycle_id>'; confirm the new headers don't accidentally match.

Extract a shared readJsonl(path: string): unknown[] helper in src/audit.ts that reads a newline-delimited JSON file, skips empty lines, and silently drops malformed lines (returning the valid ones). Refactor existing tail/filter utilities to use it. Add tests covering missing file, empty file, malformed lines mixed with valid, and trailing newline.

Add 'generalstaff history --format=json' for machine-readable cycle history output. Emit an array of cycle objects with { cycle_id, project_id, started_at, outcome, diff_stats, reason }. Parse-able by external tools. Add tests.

Add a reviewer provider fallback chain. In src/reviewer.ts, if the primary provider (from GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER) returns a response starting with '[REVIEWER ERROR]', retry once using the provider named in GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER (env var). Log the fallback attempt via appendProgress. Skip fallback if unset or equal to primary. Add tests for: fallback triggered, fallback not triggered (primary succeeded), fallback also fails, fallback equals primary (no retry).

Validate 'generalstaff task add' input: reject empty titles or whitespace-only titles with a clear error message ('task title cannot be empty') and non-zero exit. Add tests covering valid title, empty string, whitespace-only, and very long titles (accept but warn if over 500 chars).

Add a 'generalstaff log --grep=<pattern>' flag that filters log output by a case-insensitive regex pattern against the event+data fields of PROGRESS.jsonl. Rejects invalid regex with a clear error. Works alongside --lines/--project/--level. Add tests.

Add a formatFileCount(n: number) helper to src/format.ts that pluralizes: 0 -> '0 files', 1 -> '1 file', 2 -> '2 files'. Use it in writeDigest's 'What got done' list (replacing the current '${n} file(s)' template). Add tests covering 0, 1, 2, large numbers, and negative (should return '0 files').

Extract fetchCommitSubject from src/session.ts into a new module src/git.ts with its own tests. session.ts imports from there. The new module should be a natural home for future git helpers (countCommitsAhead currently lives in cycle.ts and could also move here in a later task).

Add a test for invokeOllamaReviewer verifying it correctly returns the message.content when the response also contains a 'thinking' field (some reasoning models like qwen3 populate both). Mock fetch to return { message: { content: 'real answer', thinking: 'internal reasoning' } } and verify the result is 'real answer' (thinking field ignored).

Add a 'generalstaff task count [--project=<id>]' subcommand that reports the number of pending vs done tasks per project (or for all projects when --project is omitted). Output format: 'myproj: 5 pending, 12 done (17 total)'. Add tests.

(BUG) Investigate why writeDigest's first cycle of a session sometimes falls back to cycle_id instead of the commit subject in the 'What got done' list. The 2026-04-17 morning run's digest showed cycle 1 as '20260417100252_4z21' instead of 'gs-087: add parseDigest tests' even though the commit landed cleanly. Reproduce with a git fixture: init repo, commit A, branch bot/work, commit B on bot/work, merge B into main, then call fetchCommitSubject(A, B) and expect B's subject. If the fixture test passes, instrument session.ts's cycle_start_sha / cycle_end_sha assignment for the first cycle â€” the bug may be that cycle 1's start SHA equals the session-init-commit SHA created at session start, and the cycle_end_sha reflects post-auto-merge state rather than the bot's own commit. Add regression test either way.

Add a 'generalstaff session --exclude-project=<id>[,<id>...]' filter. When the project picker (src/dispatcher.ts pickNextProject) evaluates candidates, skip any project id in the exclude set. Parse comma-separated values from the flag. Useful for 'skip this project for tonight's run'. Add tests covering: single exclude, multiple excludes, excluded project is the only eligible project (session ends with no-project), excluded project id doesn't exist (warn but don't error).

Add a 'session_complete' ProgressEntry event type (fleet-level, not per-project) that emits once at session end with aggregated stats: total_cycles, total_verified, total_failed, duration_minutes, stop_reason. Complements the existing per-project session_end entries. Add to ProgressEventType union in types.ts, emit from session.ts after the per-project session_end loop, and add tests verifying the event fires exactly once per session.

Add a 'generalstaff status --watch[=N]' flag that refreshes the status output every N seconds until killed (Ctrl-C). Default N=5 when --watch is specified without a value. Minimum N=1. Use setInterval and clear the terminal between renders (ANSI clear-screen, suppressed when not a TTY â€” fall back to repeated output). Add tests with a mocked timer covering: default interval, custom interval, interval clamping at 1.

Extract the result-categorization logic from src/session.ts (the verified/failed/skipped filtering used in the notification block and session-end logging) into a helper `categorizeResults(results: CycleResult[]): { verified: CycleResult[]; failed: CycleResult[]; skipped: CycleResult[] }` in a new src/results.ts module. verified_weak counts as verified (per existing convention). Refactor session.ts and src/summary.ts to use it. Add tests covering each bucket plus edge cases (empty array, all one type).

Include the reviewer provider name in the digest header so multi-provider runs are traceable from the digest alone. Read from GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER (default 'claude') at session start, thread it through writeDigest's config param, and emit a '**Reviewer:** <provider>' line in the digest header (e.g., '**Reviewer:** ollama (qwen3:8b)'). When GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_MODEL is set, include the model in parentheses. Add tests covering default, openrouter, ollama, and unknown-provider cases.

Add a pre-flight Ollama reachability check in session.ts when GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER=ollama. Before the first cycle runs, fire a GET to `${OLLAMA_HOST}/api/tags` (default http://localhost:11434). If unreachable, emit a warning to stderr suggesting the user either start the Ollama server (`ollama serve`) or set GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=openrouter. Non-fatal â€” the session proceeds; bad-call fallback will catch it at cycle time anyway, but the pre-flight warning gives clearer error attribution. Add tests mocking fetch for reachable/unreachable/timeout cases.

Include per-cycle engineer duration in the 'Cycle N completed' log line so users can spot slowdowns in real time. Change `Cycle N completed: generalstaff â€” verified (58m50s remaining)` to `Cycle N completed: generalstaff â€” verified (took 6m12s, 58m50s remaining)`. Compute duration from cycle.started_at and cycle.ended_at. Add test.

Remove the now-dead Telegram notify invocation from scripts/run_session.bat. src/notify.ts handles end-of-session notification directly from bun now (see 2026-04-17 notify refactor), so the powershell call in the .bat is redundant (and unreachable in detached launch contexts anyway). Keep scripts/notify_telegram.ps1 for manual re-sends. Update the .bat's comment block to reflect the new flow.

Add a test for invokeReviewerWithFallback covering the Ollama-unreachable-to-OpenRouter-fallback scenario: primary=ollama returns '[REVIEWER ERROR] Ollama fetch failed...', fallback=openrouter returns a well-formed response, verify final rawResponse is the openrouter content AND the onFallback callback fired with the primary error. Uses mocked fetch.

Enforce Hard Rule 5 (mandatory hands-off lists) with a pre-flight validator in src/projects.ts loadProjectsYaml: if any registered project has an empty or missing hands_off array, reject the config at load time with a clear error pointing at the offending project id and Rule 5. Add tests covering: valid config, missing hands_off, empty hands_off, multiple projects where one is invalid.

Add test coverage for the session_complete ProgressEntry event (introduced by gs-099): verify exactly one event fires per session_end path, data.total_cycles matches allResults.length, data.stop_reason is in the expected set ('budget'|'max-cycles'|'stop-file'|'no-project'|'insufficient-budget'|'empty-cycles'), data.duration_minutes is a non-negative number. Use a session fixture or mock.

Produce a README outline proposal at `docs/README-PROPOSAL.md` following the matiassingers/awesome-readme checklist structure. Read README.md (existing), CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, PIVOT-2026-04-15.md, FUTURE-DIRECTIONS-2026-04-15.md, RULE-RELAXATION-2026-04-15.md, UI-VISION-2026-04-15.md, and the PHASE-1-*-2026-04-15 docs. Output section headers + one-bullet-per-section summary + cite which source doc each bullet draws from. Do NOT modify README.md itself. Do NOT write any narrative prose in the sections. Only propose structure and what-belongs-where. This is pure retrieval/outlining on behalf of the README dogfooding experiment proposed in FUTURE-DIRECTIONS Â§6 â€” the user will handle voice, positioning, and final prose in a later interactive session. Create the docs/ directory if it doesn't exist.

Augment docs/README-PROPOSAL.md with voice-calibration annotations. Read VOICE.md in full (all sections including 'Mainstream-economics anchors' and 'Dogfooding as credibility'). For each proposed section in README-PROPOSAL.md, append a one-line `_Voice:_` annotation that flags where the editorial voice calibration applies â€” e.g., section Â§5 'The problem' â†’ '_Voice: lean into anti-slop + anti-extraction framing from VOICE.md Â§1; optional Falk & Tsoukalas anchor._'; section Â§11 'Why this over the alternatives' â†’ '_Voice: direct Polsia comparison allowed per VOICE.md Â§1; keep pointed not screed per Â§2; dogfooding contrast is structural not rhetorical per VOICE.md Â§Dogfooding._'; section Â§13 'Hammerstein framing' â†’ '_Voice: the Marxist-compatible reading per VOICE.md Â§Intellectual framing is fair game._'; section Â§3 'Status / Maturity' â†’ '_Voice: add dogfooding credibility line per VOICE.md Â§Dogfooding README placement._'; section Â§6 'The approach / How it works' â†’ '_Voice: reference PROGRESS.jsonl as observable evidence per VOICE.md Â§Dogfooding README placement._'; neutral sections (badges, prerequisites, license) â†’ '_Voice: neutral technical._'. Do NOT rewrite any section headers or bullets. Do NOT write the actual README prose. Only add the inline `_Voice:_` lines.

(BUG) When the engineer subprocess exits abnormally (exit code null, meaning killed by signal, OR any non-zero exit code), the cycle should skip the verification gate + reviewer entirely and mark itself verification_failed with reason 'engineer exited abnormally (code=X)'. Currently src/cycle.ts proceeds through verification on whatever partial diff exists, which accepted a killed-mid-task engineer's partial work as verified on 2026-04-17 cycle 10. Add early-return in executeCycle after engineer completes if engineerResult.exitCode === null || engineerResult.exitCode !== 0. Add tests covering: exit=0 (proceeds), exit=1 (blocks), exit=null i.e. killed (blocks), with verification of the cycle_end event's reason field.

Add a session-level watchdog that logs a warning when a cycle takes >2x its cycle_budget_minutes. Purpose: give users a visible signal that an engineer or reviewer step is stuck before the engineer timeout fires. Implementation: in session.ts, after executeCycle returns, compute actual duration from result.started_at / result.ended_at and compare to project.cycle_budget_minutes * 2. Emit console.error('[WATCHDOG] cycle X took Nm, 2x the budget â€” consider investigating') when threshold exceeded. Non-fatal, purely observability. Add test.

Add `generalstaff digest --regen=<digest-file-path>` that re-renders an existing digest markdown from the underlying PROGRESS.jsonl cycle_end events. Useful for recovering when a digest rendered with a bug (like the 2026-04-17 cycle-1 subject fallback) needs a fresh pass with the corrected code. Reads the digest file's date range, finds matching cycle_end entries, rebuilds CycleResult objects, calls writeDigest. Writes to a new timestamped file rather than overwriting. Add tests.

Add a regression test for the engineer timeout Windows process-tree kill (src/engineer.ts killChildTree). Since testing the actual spawn+kill path is cross-platform-flaky in a unit test, instead assert that on process.platform === 'win32', the code path uses spawnSync('taskkill', ['/pid', ..., '/f', '/t']) rather than child.kill('SIGTERM'). Mock spawnSync and verify the call shape. Also verify that on non-Windows platforms the signal path is used.

Add end-to-end test coverage for notifySessionEnd's real homedir loader path (currently only the dependency-injection path is tested in tests/notify.test.ts). Use a fixture ~/.claude layout inside a tmpdir, point HOME / USERPROFILE env at it, verify loadTelegramCredentials reads the correct files. Restore env after. Add a cross-platform skip marker if HOME env manipulation is too brittle on Windows CI.

Investigate and fix `.bot-worktree` cleanup after abnormal cycle termination. When a cycle is killed (engineer timeout, manual taskkill, STOP file), does cleanupWorktree() still fire? If not, stale worktrees accumulate and the next cycle's `git worktree add` fails. Add a pre-cycle cleanup pass that removes any existing `.bot-worktree` before `git worktree add`. Add tests covering: clean run (no stale worktree), stale worktree present (removed + replaced), worktree locked by another process (warn but don't crash).

Add a `generalstaff doctor --fix` mode. Currently `doctor` only diagnoses; the `--fix` mode prompts (y/N) before each auto-resolvable issue: missing state directories â†’ create, stale `.bot-worktree` â†’ remove, orphaned STOP file from crashed session â†’ remove (with confirmation). Never touches user code or git refs. Add tests for each fix path using fixture project roots.

Add validateConfig(yaml) pre-flight in src/projects.ts that runs BEFORE a session starts and rejects invalid configs with a clear error listing every problem at once (don't fail on first). Checks: project paths exist, hands_off non-empty (Hard Rule 5), engineer_command set, verification_command set, cycle_budget_minutes > 0, branch name valid. Emit errors to stderr with the project id prefix. Add tests for each failure mode.

Add a `generalstaff stop --force` subcommand that writes the STOP file AND attempts to kill any running bun/claude processes associated with the current session. On Windows, use `taskkill /pid X /f /t` matching PIDs from running sessions (read from state if tracked). Current `stop` just writes the file, which doesn't interrupt a hung cycle. Add tests using mocked process table.

Add test coverage for parseReviewerResponse when the response contains `<think>...</think>` tags before the JSON (Qwen3 reasoning mode can emit these in Ollama responses). Expected behavior: strip the think-tags or scan past them, then parse the trailing JSON. Add fixtures covering: think-tags wrapping reasoning with JSON after, nested think-tags, think-tags that contain JSON-looking text that should NOT be parsed.

Refactor executeCycle in src/cycle.ts to consolidate its multiple early-return paths into a single assembly point. Currently there are 6+ places that construct a CycleResult with different fields set, which makes adding new fields (like the gs-111 abnormal-exit block) error-prone. Approach: use a mutable builder object throughout the function, single return at the end. Don't change behavior â€” just structure. Safety: the 600+ tests are the regression guard. Add no new tests; this is a pure refactor.

Improve the 'bot/work branch has unmerged commits' error message in src/cycle.ts to interpolate the resolved project path in the suggested git command â€” e.g., `git -C /full/path/to/project merge --no-ff bot/work` rather than the current placeholder. Also suggest the alternative `auto_merge: true` config change with the exact project id. Add a test.

Add a `generalstaff session --verbose` flag that streams PROGRESS.jsonl events to stdout as they fire (not just the cycle-completion summaries). Useful for debugging stuck cycles in real time and for demo screencasts. Format each event as one line with timestamp + event name + short data summary. Default off. Add test.

Investigate and fix scripts/wait_then_launch_ollama.ps1. Used on 2026-04-17 for the first dentist-run chain attempt â€” it polled correctly but never fired the child Start-Process when the idle threshold was met. Reproduce with a fixture log directory + short threshold, determine root cause, fix, add smoke test.

Design doc: the session-budget vs cycle-budget reservation math. A 60-min session only allows 25 usable min of cycles (60 - 35-min reservation). Document in DESIGN.md. Propose one of: (a) lower cycle_budget_minutes since real cycles are 4-8 min, (b) adapt based on moving-average cycle duration, (c) document as-is. No code.

Add `generalstaff session --chain=<N>` flag that queues N back-to-back sessions, each starting after the previous finishes. Same budget/reviewer across children. Enables unattended multi-hour runs past the 10-cycle-per-session cap. Add tests for --chain=0 (error), --chain=1 (equivalent to no flag), --chain=3.
Add `generalstaff status --sessions` flag: table of last N sessions (start time, duration, cycle count, pass/fail, reviewer, stop reason). Reads session_complete PROGRESS events. Default N=10. Tests.

Do NOT modify src/safety.ts or src/reviewer.ts; the fix lives entirely in src/cycle.ts, src/session.ts, src/types.ts.

(BUG) Reviewer producing false-positive hands_off_violations. If dropping violations leaves no real violations AND no other failure signals (scope_drift_files, silent_failures), flip the verdict from verification_failed to verified. Add tests in tests/cycle.test.ts covering: (a) reviewer reports a file IS in the diff â€” violation stands, (b) reviewer reports a file NOT in the diff â€” violation dropped, hallucination event emitted, (c) all violations hallucinated AND no other failures â€” verdict flips to verified, (d) mixed real + hallucinated â€” real stands, hallucinated dropped. title: gs-280 â€” **Verification-gate JSON syntax check.** Root cause of 2026-04-20 cascading bug: bot's engineer edit to state/<project>/tasks.json left the file malformed (missing `},` between sibling task objects). Fix: in src/cycle.ts before reviewer runs, or as a new preflight gate, run `JSON.parse` on every `.json` file in the diff. Does NOT touch src/verification.ts (keep the external verification command untouched). Expected diff: ~60-100 lines across src/cycle.ts + tests/cycle.test.ts.
